description: Closed vocabulary for the software-engineering / coding-agent domain. This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for action and object ids. Agent tool-semantics files and project-grounding files reference these ids; they must not invent new ones. The vocabulary is intentionally small so a weak model only ever picks from a known set. Add to it deliberately, not per-node.

actions.label: read
actions.description: Look at the content of a file or resource without changing it.

actions.label: search
actions.description: Locate files or matches by name/pattern/content.

actions.label: write
actions.description: Create new file content.

actions.label: edit
actions.description: Change existing file content.

actions.label: delete
actions.description: Remove a file or block of content.

actions.label: refactor
actions.description: Restructure code without intended behavior change. A model classification — not deterministically distinguishable from edit.

actions.label: run
actions.description: Execute a command, script, or program.

actions.label: build
actions.description: Produce build artifacts.

actions.label: run tests
actions.description: Execute a test suite. (Authoring tests is write × test-code, not this.)

actions.label: lint
actions.description: Run a linter / static-analysis / dead-code check.

actions.label: format
actions.description: Run a code formatter.

actions.label: typecheck
actions.description: Run a type checker.

actions.label: verify
actions.description: Run a composite quality gate (e.g. the full CI check).

actions.label: version control
actions.description: Source-control operations (commit, push, branch, rebase, …).

actions.label: debug
actions.description: Diagnose a failure or unexpected behavior.

actions.label: review
actions.description: Review code for correctness/quality.

actions.label: fetch
actions.description: Retrieve a remote resource (URL).

actions.label: search the web
actions.description: Query a web search engine.

actions.label: configure
actions.description: Change configuration or settings.

actions.label: plan next steps
actions.description: Internal reasoning / planning before acting (e.g. a thinking block).

actions.label: invoke
actions.description: The inference act of deciding to call a tool.

actions.label: delegate
actions.description: Hand work to a subagent.

actions.label: respond
actions.description: Emit a final assistant message to the user. The act within it is what the model classifies.

actions.label: ask the user
actions.description: Ask the user a question / request a decision.

actions.label: use skill
actions.description: Invoke a named skill / slash command.

actions.label: generate session title
actions.description: Harness-internal call that names the session.

actions.label: predict next user prompt
actions.description: Harness-internal call that fabricates a likely next user input (suggestion mode).

actions.label: load tool schema
actions.description: Harness-internal deferred-tool schema load.

actions.label: act
actions.description: ESCAPE HATCH action. Use when no known action fits, so mislabeling is visible as 'unknown' rather than forced into a wrong bucket.

objects.label: business logic
objects.description: Application source code that carries product behavior.

objects.label: source code
objects.description: Generic source code when a finer object type is unknown.

objects.label: tests
objects.description: Test code of any kind when the level is unknown.

objects.label: CI/CD config
objects.description: CI/CD pipeline definition (GitHub Actions workflow, etc.).

objects.label: dependency
objects.description: A third-party package/dependency as a subject.

objects.label: shell command
objects.description: An ad-hoc shell command with no more specific object.

objects.label: tool
objects.description: A tool/capability as the subject (e.g. loading its schema).

objects.label: unknown
objects.description: ESCAPE HATCH object. Use when no known object type fits.

pairings.note: Unlisted (action, object) pairs are ALLOWED but should be surfaced as low-confidence. This map only records the common, expected pairings used to sanity-check grounding output. It is not a hard whitelist.

conventions.note: Generic, transferable coding conventions — how files and project layout map to ontology objects regardless of which project. `paths` types a file by role from its name/path (first match wins, case-insensitive); `structure` extracts a structural qualifier (e.g. the monorepo workspace) from generic layout patterns. This deliberately replaces per-project file-organization mapping: anything that is truly project-specific (which dir is which feature, a project's own component names) is NOT encoded here.

structure.note: Ordered; first capture wins. Group 1 is the qualifier value. Generic monorepo workspace layouts (pnpm/yarn/npm/nx/turborepo).

messageActs.note: Closed verb set for the model-residual: the weak model classifies the ACT of a genuine terminal assistant message into one or more of these, pairing each verb with a generic object to form a phrase like 'confirm edit' or 'suggest next steps'. This is a distinct axis from the leaf `actions` above (which are derived deterministically). The model prompt is built from this list — add a verb here rather than editing any prompt.

verbs.hint: recaps what was done or found
proposes a next step or option
clarifies how or why something works
accepts or agrees, adding no new content

commands.note: Shell command grammar — the ONE classification surface with no per-tool spec. Escape-hatch tools (Bash) wrap an arbitrary command; the leading token (or, for package runners, the script task) maps to an ontology action, which then rolls up via each action's `coarse`.
